description: Daisy enters Area 51 in disguise, confronting Sousa and asserting her authority as a CIA agent to secure the release of Coulson and Simmons. Displaying quick thinking, Daisy uses Sousa's background and exploits his desire to stop infiltration to convince him, even as Sousa tries to verify her bona fides. Their verbal duel is tense but Daisy ultimately earns his cooperation—just as the situation escalates elsewhere.

transcript.line: It was a test.
And you passed. Flying colors.
Well, it's nice to finally meet the man in charge.
Gosh, you just go right to that, huh? And so, man to man, Helius, is it secure? ‘Cause if there were any weaknesses, it'd be key if we could collab on that.
Oh, sure. But in order for that to happen, I would have to be a moron!
No, just wait... Wait, wait, wait, wait. We were interfacing so well.
Tell your pinko friends to go screw! And as for Helius, that hunk-o-junk's about as functional as a paperweight. Nothing on Earth is powerful enough to get it up to velocity. So take it, Stalinite.
It doesn't work.
That's right, Ivan. Yeah. Yeah, run back to the Urals!
So, Helius is controlled from the lab, but kept a safe distance away from the buildings.
I got something
Of course you did.
Helius is a dud. It doesn't work. He said there's nothing on Earth powerful enough to get it up to velocity.
That's a good thing, right?

No, but he's wrong, because there is now... a Chronicom. Remember when Original-Enoch fueled up the monolith machine and went all supernova and I didn't know if I was gonna die and it... It was very scary.
Wait, so you think Helius could be powered up the same way?
A Chronicom that's willing to die plus an Ion Fusion Reactor will equal a bomb with a blast radius for miles. If that thing goes off, it could take out...
The entire brain trust of S.H.I.E.L.D. We need to call in the Cavalry. Well, that's what we call May.
But isn't she still an unfeeling killbot?
That's exactly what we're facing. Might be exactly what we need.
